commands.review_summary:
Adds an `on-request` Codex native plugin destructive-action policy, routes matched MCP approval elicitations through OpenClaw plugin approvals, and updates config, migration, docs, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +168, Tests +422, Docs +4. Total +594 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a current-main bug report, and the PR body supplies after-fix live Slack/Codex/Google Calendar proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No mechanical repair is indicated; the next action is human maintainer review of the config/API and approval-semantics change.

target.title: feat(codex): add on-request plugin approvals
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `on-request` as a Codex plugin approval setting so MCP elicitations can be surfaced as interactive OpenClaw plugin approvals instead of being auto-accepted or auto-denied.
- Routes Codex app-server elicitation approval requests through the plugin approval flow, including session-bound channel delivery and roundtrip approval handling.
- Updates Codex plugin docs, config parsing, migration defaults, and focused tests for `always-allow`, `always-deny`, and `on-request` behavior.
- Success means a native Codex plugin MCP write can pause on approval, show Slack approval buttons, proceed after `Allow Once`, and complete the underlying connector action.

## Linked context

Requested by maintainer in this Codex session for Codex plugin MCP elicitations and `on-request` config behavior.

Related local spec/work: Codex plugin elicitations to plugin approvals.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex Google Calendar native plugin `create_event` with Codex plugin approval config set to `on-request` now surfaces an OpenClaw plugin approval in Slack and proceeds only after user approval.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw `codex-dev` profile, Slack channel delivery, native Codex Google Calendar connector, `kevin@dendron.so` calendar account.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw --profile codex-dev gateway run --force --ws-log compact`; then `pnpm openclaw --profile codex-dev agent --agent dev --session-key agent:dev:codex-on-request-create-event-accessibility-1781275534 --channel slack --to U04HRD671T2 --message "Use Google Calendar create_event immediately: calendar_id primary, title 'Walk lobster', start_time '2026-06-12T23:30:00-07:00', end_time '2026-06-13T00:00:00-07:00', timezone_str 'America/Los_Angeles', attendees []." --reply-channel slack --reply-to U04HRD671T2 --thinking off --json --timeout 600`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Slack posted `Plugin approval required` for approval ID `plugin:516ce5b5-e027-4587-a6ee-847282bab742` with `Allow Once`, `Allow Always`, and `Deny`; after `Allow Once`, the Codex run completed with `Created Walk lobster on your primary calendar for June 12, 2026, 11:30 PM to June 13, 2026, 12:00 AM Pacific. Event ID: 62gktmukfqoqjrfujljgg1bv8k`. Google Calendar connector search returned event id `62gktmukfqoqjrfujljgg1bv8k`, summary `Walk lobster`, start `2026-06-12T23:30:00-07:00`, end `2026-06-13T00:00:00-07:00`.
- Observed result after fix: The Codex plugin write did not auto-run; it waited for Slack approval, then created the Calendar event after approval.
- What was not tested: Browser-side Google Calendar event detail page for `kevin@dendron.so`; Arc/Safari required Google re-auth for that account, so I stopped before entering credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Browser UI could not access the connector account without re-auth. The connector itself verified the created event by ID and time.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Earlier attempts without timely approval showed `user rejected MCP tool call` / expired approval and did not create an event.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-thread-config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-activation.test.ts` - 5 files passed, 177 tests passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Live OpenClaw/Codex/Slack/Google Calendar proof described above.

Regression coverage was added around Codex app-server approval config parsing, elicitation bridge approval behavior, plugin thread config, session binding, and plugin activation defaults.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Codex plugin MCP elicitations can now ask the user via plugin approval UI when configured as `on-request`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The Codex plugin approval config accepts `on-request`, and migration/config defaults were updated.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Tool execution approval behavior changes for Codex native plugin MCP tools.

What is the highest-risk area?

Approval semantics around write-capable native plugin MCP tools.

How is that risk mitigated?

The new mode is explicit in config, is covered by focused unit tests, and was live-tested through Slack approval before a Google Calendar write executed.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI is pending after PR creation.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet.
